page: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is a virtual machine instance belonging to the High Frequency Compute server family. It features 1 shared vCPU based on the x86_64 architecture from Intel, with 1 physical core and 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

Storage is bundled with the instance, consisting of a 64 GB local NVMe SSD. The server configuration also includes 1 complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this instance.

Because the CPU allocation is shared, CPU performance may vary depending on host node utilization, representing a tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained maximum compute power. Qualitatively, this instance offers a low-cost entry point for resource-constrained applications. It is suitable for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, small development environments, microservices, and low-traffic utility applications that do not require dedicated CPU resources.
description: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is a budget-friendly virtual server within the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 1 shared Intel vCPU on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware profile provides 2.0 GB of system memory alongside 64 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering a balanced setup for low-intensity tasks. No dedicated GPU hardware is included. Because the CPU allocation is shared, performance may vary depending on host node load, making this instance less suitable for sustained high-compute workloads. It serves as a cost-efficient option for lightweight applications, including basic web serving, microservices, utility scripts, and small development environments. The server also comes equipped with one complimentary public IPv4 address.
og_description: Vultr vhf-1c-2gb is a High Frequency Compute instance featuring 1 shared Intel vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 64 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight and budget-conscious workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring a shared Intel processor and local NVMe SSD storage for lightweight utility workloads.
